db_statements: only one among them, a certain Mírzá Ḥusayn-i-Mutavallíy-i-Qumí, showed any desire to eat of the food the sovereign had chosen to spread before us.

We were awakened one night, ere break of day, by Mírzá ‘Abdu’l-Vahháb-i-S̱hírází, who was bound with Us to the same chains. He had left Káẓimayn and followed Us to Ṭihrán, where he was arrested and thrown into prison.
That morning saw the gaoler again enter Our cell and call out the name of ‘Abdu’l-Vahháb.

A youth named ‘Abbás, a former servant of Ḥájí Sulaymán Ḵhán, and fully informed... of the names, the number, and the dwelling places of the Báb’s disciples, was employed by the enemy as an instrument... He had identified himself with the Faith of his master.
He was arrested and compelled to betray all those whom he knew to be associated with the Faith.

canonical_name: Mírzá Áqá Khán-i-Núrí, the I‘timádu’d-Dawlih (Grand Vizir)

The Báb’s only chance of meeting Muḥammad S̱háh — a meeting which He Himself had requested and on which He had pinned His fondest hopes — was dashed to the ground by the intervention of the cowardly and capricious Ḥájí Mírzá Áqásí.
Muḥammad S̱háh was seriously ill at the time of this disaster.

a certain siyyid whose chequered career of service was brought to a sudden end in Luristán by the action of the treacherous Íldirím Mírzá.
